headline: Xcel Energy (XEL) and Avangrid Extend Power Purchase Agreement for 150MW Wind Project
summary: Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ:XEL) is one of the best NASDAQ stocks to buy according to hedge funds. On February 25, Xcel Energy reached an agreement with Avangrid to extend its existing Power Purchase Agreement for the MinnDakota wind energy project. This extension ensures that Xcel will continue to be the sole offtaker of the electricity […]

headline: American Electric Power (AEP) Names Adrian Rodriguez President and COO of AEP Texas
summary: American Electric Power Company Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) is one of the best NASDAQ stocks to buy according to hedge funds. On March 6, American Electric Power, or simply AEP, announced that Adrian Rodriguez has been named president and chief operating officer of AEP Texas. Joining the company on March 30, Rodriguez will report directly to AEP […]

headline: Assessing Xcel Energy (XEL) Valuation As Recent Share Moves Contrast With Mixed Fair Value Signals
summary: Why Xcel Energy (XEL) Is On Investors’ Radar Today Xcel Energy (XEL) is drawing fresh attention after recent share moves, with the stock showing a 0.5% decline over the past day and a 2% decline over the past week. See our latest analysis for Xcel Energy. While the 1-day and 7-day share price returns are slightly negative, Xcel Energy’s 30-day and 90-day share price returns of 8.2% and 8.4% indicate that momentum has picked up recently, contributing to a 1-year total shareholder return of...

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Xcel Energy (XEL) After 22% One-Year Share Price Gain?
summary: If you are wondering whether Xcel Energy is still fairly priced after its recent run, this breakdown will help you understand what you might actually be paying for today. The stock recently closed at US$82.10, with returns of 8.2% over the last 30 days and 22.2% over the past year, which may change how investors think about both its growth potential and its risk profile. Recent news around Xcel Energy has focused on its role as a regulated utility and ongoing attention on the sector,...

headline: How The Xcel Energy (XEL) Story Is Shifting With Data Centers And Mixed Analyst Targets
summary: Xcel Energy’s fair value estimate has shifted slightly, moving from $88.65 to $88.94 per share, while some bullish analysts are now using price targets such as $91 in their updated work on regulated and diversified utilities. Those changes sit alongside more cautious revisions from bearish voices, who have trimmed targets in recent months as they weigh sector sentiment, affordability questions, and regulatory risk. In the sections that follow, you will see how this mix of target moves and...
